# Dyadpy

> Dyadpy is a type-safe RPC bridge between Python and TypeScript. The
> Python function signature is the contract — Dyadpy extracts an IR from
> your handlers via `inspect` + `typing.get_type_hints` + `msgspec`'s
> JSON Schema export, then writes a single typed `client.ts` into your
> frontend. No DTO duplication, no OpenAPI round-trip, no `Body_create_post_posts_post`.

Dyadpy ships at the wire level: HTTP routes, body/param markers, typed
streaming via SSE, typed errors as discriminated unions, post-response
hooks, dependency injection. Nothing higher-level (no auth, no rate
limiting, no LLM types in core). The IR is JSON Schema 2020-12 plus a
thin Dyadpy layer for streams/errors/tasks, so other languages (Swift,
Kotlin, anything that can read JSON Schema) can consume the same
contract.

Two halves on disk: `dyadpy` (Python, PyPI) is the framework + CLI +
codegen; `@dyadpy/ts` (npm, ~3 KB) is the framework-agnostic runtime
your generated `client.ts` imports. Framework adapters
(`@dyadpy/react`, `@dyadpy/svelte`, `@dyadpy/solid`) wrap the client in
idiomatic primitives.

## Docs

- [Getting started](https://github.com/tamimbinhakim/dyadpy/blob/main/docs/getting-started.md): five-minute install + write a handler + see the generated client.
- [Reference](https://github.com/tamimbinhakim/dyadpy/blob/main/docs/reference.md): every primitive Dyadpy exports, with the smallest working example for each.
- [Architecture](https://github.com/tamimbinhakim/dyadpy/blob/main/docs/architecture.md): what happens when you type `dyadpy dev` — IR extraction, ASGI dispatch, codegen, dev loop.
- [Design](https://github.com/tamimbinhakim/dyadpy/blob/main/docs/design.md): the "why" behind every "what" — why msgspec, why SSE, why no OpenAPI by default.
- [Auth recipes](https://github.com/tamimbinhakim/dyadpy/blob/main/docs/auth.md): JWT, sessions, NextAuth — wiring `Depends(current_user)` to your provider.
- [SSR](https://github.com/tamimbinhakim/dyadpy/blob/main/docs/ssr.md): server-side rendering with Next.js App Router, SvelteKit, and Solid Start — prefetch + hydrate helpers, header forwarding, the CSR-safety contract.
- [IR stability](https://github.com/tamimbinhakim/dyadpy/blob/main/docs/ir-stability.md): additive-only invariants on the JSON-Schema IR and the on-the-wire envelope.
- [Semver policy](https://github.com/tamimbinhakim/dyadpy/blob/main/docs/semver.md): what counts as a break and the deprecation cycle.
- [LTS policy](https://github.com/tamimbinhakim/dyadpy/blob/main/docs/lts.md): support windows, backport policy, EOL rules.

## Source

- [packages/dyadpy](https://github.com/tamimbinhakim/dyadpy/tree/main/packages/dyadpy): Python framework, CLI, IR builder, codegen.
- [packages/dyadpy-ts](https://github.com/tamimbinhakim/dyadpy/tree/main/packages/dyadpy-ts): framework-agnostic TS runtime — `createClient`, `parseSSE`, `Result<T, E>`.
- [packages/dyadpy-react](https://github.com/tamimbinhakim/dyadpy/tree/main/packages/dyadpy-react): React hooks (`useQuery`, `useMutation`, `useSubscription`) on TanStack Query.
- [packages/dyadpy-svelte](https://github.com/tamimbinhakim/dyadpy/tree/main/packages/dyadpy-svelte): Svelte 5 store bindings.
- [packages/dyadpy-solid](https://github.com/tamimbinhakim/dyadpy/tree/main/packages/dyadpy-solid): SolidJS resource bindings.

## Examples

- [examples/nextjs-streaming](https://github.com/tamimbinhakim/dyadpy/tree/main/examples/nextjs-streaming): Next.js App Router consuming a streaming endpoint.
- [examples/vite-react](https://github.com/tamimbinhakim/dyadpy/tree/main/examples/vite-react): minimal Vite + React app with hooks.
- [examples/sveltekit-counter](https://github.com/tamimbinhakim/dyadpy/tree/main/examples/sveltekit-counter): SvelteKit + Svelte stores.

## Optional

- [README](https://github.com/tamimbinhakim/dyadpy/blob/main/README.md): the elevator pitch, comparison table, philosophy.
- [Roadmap](https://github.com/tamimbinhakim/dyadpy/blob/main/ROADMAP.md): what's planned and why.
- [Contributing](https://github.com/tamimbinhakim/dyadpy/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md): how to file issues, send PRs, run tests locally.
- [Releasing](https://github.com/tamimbinhakim/dyadpy/blob/main/RELEASING.md): the release process — `release-please`, PyPI + npm publishing.
- [Full docs (single file)](https://github.com/tamimbinhakim/dyadpy/blob/main/llms-full.txt): every doc page concatenated, for LLMs with large contexts.
